name: Almond Trees Trail
name_original: Senda de los Almendros (VA-PRC-09) — circular trailrunning desde Castronuño
description: Enjoy an easy 2.1-mile (3.3 km) trail run along the Duero River in Castronuño, featuring birdwatching and a grand river meander view.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Sur offer a diverse landscape, characterized by gentle river valleys and agricultural areas, often featuring historical landmarks. The terrain typically includes paved paths and well-maintained trails, with some routes traversing open fields and offering views of local architecture. Elevation changes are generally mild, making the area suitable for various fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Sur The most popular jogging route is Castle of La Mota Loop, a 2.5 miles (4.1 km) trail that takes 25 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the historic castle and surrounding landscape. Another top favourite among local runners is Almond Trees Trail, an easy 2.1 miles (3.3 km) path. This trail winds through scenic agricultural fields, providing a pleasant and gentle running experience. Local runners also love the Mirador al Río Duero – Iglesia de Santiago loop from Tordesillas, a 6.3 miles (10.1 km) trail leading through river views and historical townscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 3 minutes. Jogging around Sur is defined by riverine landscapes, historical town centers, and agricultural paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance runs. The routes in Sur are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from over 3 reviews. More than 570 runners have used komoot to explore Sur's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Sur?
A: There are over 40 running routes around Sur, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include 6 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 6 more challenging options, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging routes in Sur?
A: The jogging routes in Sur feature a mix of terrains. You'll find paved paths and well-maintained trails, especially along the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Sur Corniche&lt;/strong&gt;. Many routes also traverse gentle river valleys and agricultural areas, with some sections through open fields. For a different experience, some beaches offer stretches of sand suitable for running, and the broader region includes more rugged, rocky paths in the wadis and mountains for those seeking a challenge.

Q: Are there any easy or beginner-friendly jogging routes in Sur?
A: Yes, Sur offers several easy routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed run. A great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1119518397/almond-trees-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almond Trees Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 2.1-mile (3.3 km) path that winds through scenic agricultural fields, providing a pleasant and gentle running experience. Another accessible choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120211519/castle-of-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 2.5-mile (4.1 km) trail with mild elevation changes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or attractions I might see while running in Sur?
A: Many running routes in Sur offer views of historical landmarks and natural beauty. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120211519/castle-of-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt; provides views of the historic castle. Other routes may pass by significant sites such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5269241" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Historic Center of Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5834417" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Church of Santa María la Mayor de la Asunción de Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt;, or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5509532" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San José Dam&lt;/a&gt;, offering a blend of history and nature.

Q: Are there any long-distance running trails for more experienced runners?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking longer distances, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of San Antolín – Royal Testamentary Palace loop from Medina del Campo AV&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 12.2-mile (19.7 km) route. Other moderate options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador al Río Duero – Iglesia de Santiago loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt;, at 6.3 miles (10.1 km), also provide a good workout with river views and historical townscapes.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Sur?
A: The running routes in Sur are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars. Runners often praise the diverse riverine landscapes, the charm of historical town centers, and the peaceful agricultural paths. The variety of routes, from easy strolls to more challenging long-distance runs, is also a frequently highlighted positive aspect.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in Sur?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Sur are designed as loops, offering convenient circular paths. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120211519/castle-of-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador al Río Duero – Iglesia de Santiago loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Running loop from Balneario de Las Salinas&lt;/a&gt;, all providing a complete circuit back to your starting point.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Sur?
A: Sur's coastal location means it can get quite warm. The cooler months, typically from October to April, are ideal for outdoor activities like running. During these times, the temperatures are more moderate, making for a more comfortable and enjoyable experience. Early mornings or late afternoons are generally best to avoid the midday heat, especially in the warmer seasons.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Many routes in Sur provide picturesque views, particularly those along the coast or near the river. The &lt;strong&gt;Sur Corniche&lt;/strong&gt; offers breathtaking oceanfront vistas of traditional dhows, ancient watchtowers, and the city skyline. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39557405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador al Río Duero – Iglesia de Santiago loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt; also feature beautiful river views, allowing runners to enjoy the natural beauty of the region.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass through historical town centers?
A: Yes, Sur's running network includes routes that lead through charming historical town centers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31429350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Tordesillas Historic Center loop from Tordesillas&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, allowing you to combine your run with exploring the rich history and architecture of the town. These routes often feature views of local architecture and landmarks.

Q: Are there any running routes near natural features like wadis or beaches?
A: While the komoot routes primarily focus on riverine and agricultural landscapes, the broader Sur region is rich in natural features. The &lt;strong&gt;Sur Corniche&lt;/strong&gt; offers direct access to oceanfront running. For those willing to venture slightly further, the area boasts stunning wadis like &lt;strong&gt;Wadi Shab&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Wadi Tiwi&lt;/strong&gt;, which are excellent for hiking and offer unique natural scenery, though they present a different kind of terrain than typical running paths.

Q: What are the elevation changes like on running routes in Sur?
A: The running routes in Sur generally feature mild elevation changes, making them accessible for a wide range of abilities. Most routes are characterized by gentle inclines and declines, suitable for comfortable running. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1120211519/castle-of-la-mota-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of La Mota Loop&lt;/a&gt; has only about 27 meters of ascent, while even longer routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431037" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Collegiate Church of San Antolín – Royal Testamentary Palace loop&lt;/a&gt; have moderate ascents of around 57 meters over nearly 20 km.
tips: The natural viewpoint of La Muela Park in Castronuño is an impressive place that you cannot miss. From this privileged balcony, you can observe the great meander that the Duero River draws as it passes through Castronuño. This meander is one of the largest in Europe and is located in the heart of the Riberas de Castronuño natural reserve. The landscape is idyllic, and the river has been a silent witness to the passage of time and the history of this region. If you visit Castronuño, you should definitely explore this viewpoint and enjoy the panoramic views. Also, if you like hiking, there are interesting routes in the area. I hope you have a wonderful experience there! 😊

The San José Reservoir and Dam, also known as the Castronuño Dam, is a hydroelectric engineering project located in the middle course of the Duero River, about 2 km from the town of Castronuño.
Built between 1941 and 1945, the dam is a gravity type and has a height of 14.05 metres.

faqs.question: How long does it take to jogging Senda de los Almendros (VA-PRC-09) — circular trailrunning desde Castronuño?
faqs.answer: This circular trail is approximately 3.3 kilometers long and typically takes about 25 minutes to complete at a jogging pace. It's an easy route, perfect for a quick run or a leisurely walk.

faqs.question: Is the Senda de los Almendros suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Senda de los Almendros is rated as an easy trail. Its relatively short length and minimal elevation gain make it very accessible and suitable for beginners or those looking for a relaxed jog.

faqs.question: Where does the Senda de los Almendros trail start and where can I park?
faqs.answer: The trail conveniently begins in the urban park of La Muela in Castronuño. You can typically find parking available in the vicinity of the park, making it easy to access the trailhead.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this trail?
faqs.answer: The route follows the course of the Duero River, featuring pleasant riverbank vegetation. The terrain is generally flat and well-suited for jogging, offering a natural and scenic environment.

faqs.question: What wildlife can I see along the Senda de los Almendros?
faqs.answer: The trail is excellent for nature observation, especially birdwatching. Its proximity to the Duero River and rich riverbank vegetation means you can spot both aquatic and forest birds. There's even a bird observatory along the route to enhance your viewing experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: You'll enjoy panoramic views of one of Europe's largest river meanders, particularly from the View of the Duero River Meander from La Muela Park. The trail also passes near the San José Reservoir and Dam (Castronuño Dam), offering additional scenic views.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Senda de los Almendros?
faqs.answer: The trail is enjoyable year-round, but spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for jogging and walking, along with vibrant natural scenery. These seasons are also ideal for birdwatching.

faqs.question: Is the Senda de los Almendros dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as a responsible pet owner, please ensure your dog is kept on a leash and that you clean up after them to preserve the natural environment for everyone.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Senda de los Almendros?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Senda de los Almendros. The trail is freely accessible to the public. It is located within the Riberas de Castronuño - Vega del Duero Nature Reserve, so please respect all local regulations regarding nature conservation.

faqs.question: Does the Senda de los Almendros connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Etapa 16: Tordesillas – Castronuño, Etapa 17: Castronuño – Toro, and the Ruta del Duero, offering potential for longer explorations if desired.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like restrooms or water along the trail?
faqs.answer: As a relatively short trail, specific facilities like restrooms or dedicated water stations are not typically found directly along the route. It's advisable to use facilities in Castronuño before starting and carry your own water, especially on warmer days.

waypoints.name: View of the Duero River Meander from La Muela Park
San José Reservoir and Dam (Castronuño Dam)
